scope: Long-arc higher-fidelity force-and-density mismatch slice (Loop 47). Truth-side dynamics extend the existing higher-fidelity propagator with the dominant non-zonal sectoral spherical-harmonic terms (C22, S22) and a longitudinal/diurnal time-varying density modulation; the arc length is extended from the prior 40-minute window to a 3-hour (10800 s) window so the drag/density mismatch can accumulate observably under sparse line-of-sight visibility. The estimator side keeps the compact two-body + J2 + time-invariant exponential drag deterministic flow used by every classical baseline in the manuscript. The slice is constructed BEFORE the held-out test population is evaluated; the held-out test seed is disjoint from the validation tuning seed and from every training, model-selection, and prior test seed used in the manuscript.
truth_dynamics: Two-body + J2..J6 zonal geopotential + dominant non-zonal sectoral spherical-harmonic terms C(2,2) and S(2,2) (EGM-class normalized nominal values, rotated from Earth-fixed to the pseudo-inertial frame by the same GMST transform used throughout the manuscript) + luni-solar third-body acceleration + exponential drag with a one-sided diurnal-bulge density modulation (alpha_diurnal=0.30) and a longitudinal semidiurnal density modulation (alpha_long=0.10). The C22/S22 tesseral terms are independently checked against a finite-difference of the corresponding spherical-harmonic potential in the test suite.
estimator_dynamics: Compact two-body + J2 + time-invariant exponential-density drag (the same deterministic flow used by every classical baseline in this manuscript). The mismatch channels are therefore (i) zonal geopotential J3..J6, (ii) sectoral geopotential C22/S22 (longitudinal Earth-rotation-tracked), (iii) luni-solar third-body, (iv) time-varying drag density (diurnal + longitudinal).
rationale: Reviewer concern on Loop 46 was that the higher-fidelity force-mismatch slice at the 40-minute arc length was too short for the drag-scale structural channel to become observable. The longer 3-hour arc gives the multiplicative drag-scale parameter beta a fair window in which to be informed by sparse line-of-sight observations; the additional tesseral and longitudinal density terms guarantee the missing physics is genuinely longitudinally time-varying so the residual cannot be absorbed by a single time-invariant exponential-density shift. The same predeclared structural channels (DSA-EKF, DMC-EKF) are tested on this richer slice using the SAME augmented-state structure used in Loop 45, so the comparison to the prior 40-minute result is direct.

rule.primary_endpoint: Mean observed-step position RMSE on the held-out test population.
rule.decision_predicate: The DSA-EKF (drag-scale adaptive EKF) is a positive contribution if and only if (i) its mean observed-step position RMSE is strictly the lowest among {EKF, UKF, AUKF, PUKF, DMC-EKF, DSA-EKF} on the held-out test population, AND (ii) the paired-bootstrap 95% CI for the DSA-EKF-minus-best-non-DSA gap is strictly negative, AND (iii) the absolute gap exceeds the astrodynamics-grounded practical-significance floor (an absolute metres floor predeclared in release/predeclarations/astrodynamics_floor_loop47.json, derived only from pinned configuration quantities and the predeclared arc length).
rule.honest_negative: If the predeclared rule does not yield a positive in the sense above, it is reported as a bounded structural-channel negative on the long-arc higher-fidelity slice (no rule retuning is allowed after the held-out test evaluation).

arc.rationale_for_arc_length: Two orbital periods at a representative LEO altitude. A 3-hour arc allows the drag-induced position perturbation, which scales roughly as 0.5 * delta_a_drag * t^2, to accumulate by approximately one order of magnitude beyond the 40-minute arc used in Loop 45, giving the DSA-EKF structural channel a fair chance of becoming observable through sparse line-of-sight measurements.

validation_tuning.purpose: Select the DSA-EKF Gauss-Markov hyperparameters on a long-arc validation population BEFORE the held-out test. The validation seed is disjoint from every test, training, and model-selection split.
validation_tuning.selection_rule: Choose the grid point with the lowest mean observed-step position RMSE on the validation population; the selected hyperparameters are frozen before the held-out test.

thresholds_justification: C22/S22 EGM-class normalized nominal values are the dominant non-zonal terms of the geopotential and quantify the Earth's equatorial ellipticity. The diurnal-bulge alpha=0.30 matches the prior higher-fidelity slice so the change introduced by Loop 47 is the longer arc and the longitudinal/tesseral channel rather than a different diurnal amplitude. The longitudinal density wavenumber 2 (i.e., a semidiurnal modulation in the Earth-fixed longitude direction) is a structurally simpler placeholder for the leading semidiurnal thermospheric tide that an empirical density model would resolve; the amplitude alpha_long=0.10 keeps the perturbation small enough that the time-invariant exponential model is not catastrophically off but large enough that it cannot be absorbed by a constant rescaling. The drag-scale Gauss-Markov tau=1800 s (~one-third of an orbital period) is the standard operational LEO ballistic-coefficient estimation default extended to the longer arc; the steady-state sigma=0.5 covers approximately a 50% standard deviation around the nominal ballistic-coefficient/density product.

evaluation_protocol.data: Long-arc higher-fidelity force-and-density-mismatch slice: see scope and truth_dynamics.
evaluation_protocol.primary_endpoint: Observed-step position RMSE on a held-out test seed disjoint from the validation tuning seed.
evaluation_protocol.astrodynamics_floor_basis: Cramer-Rao lower bound on the arc-accumulated 3D position-RMSE from the configured measurement-noise standard deviations, station network geometry, and the predeclared 3-hour arc length. Derived only from pinned configuration quantities; no held-out test outcome enters the derivation.
